items.q: How do you say 'I am going to eat' in Spanish (near future)?
items.why: Students forget the 'a'. The structure is IR (conjugated) + A + INFINITIVE. The 'a' is essential and links the conjugated ir to the action. Voy comer is ungrammatical.

items.q: What goes between 'veo' and 'mi amigo' in 'I see my friend'?
items.why: Students omit the personal a. When the direct object is a SPECIFIC PERSON (or pet), Spanish inserts 'a' before it. Omitting it sounds odd. 'Veo a Juan' (not 'Veo Juan').

items.q: Which mood follows 'quiero que' (I want that)?
items.why: Students use indicative after que. Expressions of wish, emotion, doubt, or impersonal expressions trigger SUBJUNCTIVE: 'Quiero que VENGAS' (not 'que vienes'). Different subjects require que + subjunctive.

items.q: Which sentence correctly says 'I get on well with my sister'?
items.correct: Me llevo bien con mi hermana.
items.misconception: Me llevo bueno con mi hermana.
items.random_wrong: Yo llevo bien con mi hermana.
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Me llevo bien con mi hermana.. “Me llevo bueno con mi hermana.” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which is the correct use of ser/estar: 'I am tired'?
items.why: Correct answer: Estoy cansado.. “Soy cansado.” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many forms does the Spanish adjective 'simpático' have (masc sing, masc plur, fem sing, fem plur)?
items.why: The real figure is 4 forms. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
